senses.definition: to become infected with a viral illness, often characterized by symptoms such as a runny nose, cough, and congestion

examples.x: She got a cold after being exposed to someone with a contagious virus.
If you don't dress warmly in cold weather, you might get a cold.
It's common to get a cold during the flu season when many people are ill.
He got a cold from his co-worker, who was sneezing and coughing at the office.
To avoid getting a cold, it's important to practice good hygiene, such as washing your hands regularly.

senses.definition: to experience pain or discomfort in the head

examples.x: I got a headache from spending too much time staring at the computer screen.
Getting a headache can be a result of stress and tension.
Some people get a headache as a side effect of certain medications.
She got a headache after spending hours in the hot sun without a hat.
If you've get a headache, it's a good idea to rest and drink plenty of water.

senses.definition: to darken one's skin as a result of exposure to sunlight or other methods

examples.x: During their beach vacation, they spent hours in the sun to get a tan.
He got a tan while gardening in his backyard without wearing sunscreen.
Many people want to get a tan in the summer to have a sun-kissed appearance.
If you spend time at the pool or beach, it's easy to get a tan.
She decided to get a tan by using a sunbed at the tanning salon.

senses.definition: to take off what one is wearing and put on something else

examples.x: After the workout, he needed to get changed into clean clothes.
Before the party, she got changed into a beautiful evening dress.
After the outdoor activity, they got changed in the locker room.
It's common to get changed into comfortable pajamas before going to bed.
He had to get changed quickly to be ready for the formal event.
Every morning, I get dressed in my business attire before heading to the office.
After her workout, she quickly showers and gets dressed in casual clothing for the rest of the day.
The children giggled as they got dressed in their Halloween costumes for the school party.
He was running late, so he had to hurry and get dressed for the important job interview.
The actor got dressed in costume for the stage performance.

senses.definition: to remove one's clothes from one's body

examples.x: After a long day at work, she couldn't wait to get undressed and relax at home.
The doctor asked the patient to get undressed and put on a hospital gown for the examination.
In the locker room, athletes get undressed before putting on their sports attire.
Getting undressed and slipping into pajamas is a comforting bedtime routine for many.
During a medical exam, patients are usually asked to get undressed in a private area and wear a medical gown.

senses.definition: to become involved in a problematic or difficult situation, often as a result of one's actions or decisions
to legally become someone's wife or husband

examples.x: After a long courtship, they decided to get married in a beautiful outdoor ceremony.
Many couples choose to get married in the spring or summer when the weather is pleasant.
They got married at the city courthouse in a simple and intimate ceremony.
Some people prefer to get married at a destination wedding to combine the event with a vacation.
They had been together for years before they finally decided to get married.

senses.definition: to have one's hair shortened or styled by a hairdresser or barber

examples.x: She scheduled an appointment to get her hair cut and styled for a special event.
Before the start of the new school year, he wanted to get his hair cut.
Getting your hair cut regularly helps maintain its health and appearance.
He decided to get his hair cut shorter for a more comfortable summer look.
Many people prefer to get their hair cut professionally to achieve the desired style.

senses.definition: to begin a particular task, activity, or process
to understand something completely and clearly

examples.x: Before we proceed, can we get something straight regarding the project's objectives?
I want to get things straight between us; there seems to be some miscommunication.
To avoid any confusion, let's get the timeline straight for the project.
It's important to get the facts straight before making any decisions.

senses.definition: to successfully complete the required studies and receive an academic qualification from a university or college

examples.x: After four years of studying, she will finally get a degree in psychology.
He worked hard to get a degree in computer science to pursue a career in technology.
Many students aspire to get a degree in business administration for a solid foundation in management.
In order to become a teacher, it is essential to get a degree in education.
Getting a degree in journalism opened doors for her in the field of media and communication.
She's planning to take a degree in engineering next fall.
Taking a degree in computer science requires dedication and hard work.

senses.definition: to make progress or achieve success, especially when facing difficulties or obstacles
to make no progress, achieve no results, or reach no favorable outcome in a particular situation or endeavor

examples.x: Despite their efforts to negotiate, the two parties seemed to get nowhere in reaching a compromise.
He felt like he was getting nowhere in his job search, as he had applied to numerous positions without any offers.
The project's progress was slow, and the team seemed to be getting nowhere.
Their argument was going in circles, and they were getting nowhere in resolving their differences.

senses.definition: to begin considering a particular thought or idea

examples.x: He got to wondering if there was more to the story than what he had heard.
There are seeds in the soil, and I get to wondering how they transform into beautiful flowers.
After receiving no response, they got to thinking that something might be wrong.
When the phone call didn't come as expected, she got to wondering if there was a problem.
